channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ECB introduces trial allowing full player replacements in County Championship matches
item.description: The England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) has announced a season-long trial permitting full player replacements in the County Championship for cases involving injury, illness, and significant life eve...

item.title: Chalmers outlines support measures for small businesses amid Middle East crisis
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has indicated that Australia may face a recession due to the economic repercussions of the ongoing war in the Middle East, which has caused a significant disruption to global fu...

item.title: Iraq and Turkiye Secure Spots in FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Iraq have secured a historic return to the FIFA World Cup after 40 years, defeating Bolivia 2-1 in a tense intercontinental playoff held in Mexico. Coach Graham Arnold praised his squad for overcoming...

item.title: Albanese urges Australians to prepare for economic challenges amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has addressed the nation to prepare Australians for potentially difficult months ahead due to the ongoing Middle East war. While Australia is not directly involved in t...

item.title: Augustine Paul’s Contributions to Chennai’s Western Classical Music and Choral Culture
item.description: Augustine Paul, the esteemed music director of the Madras Musical Association Choir in Chennai, has long been a cornerstone of the city’s Western classical music scene. His musical journey began with ...

item.title: Officials Confirm Kidnapped Journalist Had Been Previously Warned About Threats
item.description: U.S. and Iraqi authorities have confirmed that a journalist recently abducted in Iraq had previously been made aware of specific threats to their safety. The warnings were reportedly issued before the...

item.title: APC Denies Circulating 2027 Nomination Forms Price List
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don clear say one report wey dey go round for some media, wey claim to show the cost of its nomination and expression of interest forms for 2027 elections, na false...

item.title: Challenges and Progress of Women in Zanzibar Leadership Roles
item.description: In Zanzibar, women have made some progress in politics, but the struggle for equal leadership remains. The 2026 International Women's Day, organised by TAMWA Zanzibar, highlighted both achievements an...

item.title: New 7-Episode Docuseries ‘Recharging Hope’ Highlights Stories of Resilience Across Africa
item.description: This article highlights the release of the official trailer for ‘Recharging Hope’, a seven-episode docuseries focusing on inspiring stories from across Africa. The series aims to shine a light on resi...

item.title: 13-year-old boy injured after being hit by taxi in southern Adelaide
item.description: A 13-year-old boy was struck by a taxi in the southern suburbs of Adelaide on Wednesday evening. The incident occurred in a residential area, prompting emergency services to respond swiftly. Paramedic...

item.title: FCC requests KP CM Afridi’s clarification on proposal to create force for Imran Khan’s release
item.description: On Wednesday, the Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) directed Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi to submit a response within 10 days concerning his announcement to form a special force ai...

item.title: Spanish Authorities Investigate Anti-Muslim Chants During Spain-Egypt Friendly Match
item.description: Spanish police have initiated a formal probe following Islamophobic and xenophobic chants during the Spain-Egypt international friendly football match held in Barcelona. The incident took place at the...

item.title: Kenyan MPs Advocate for National Discussion on Cancer Prevention and Research
item.description: Nairobi — Kenyan MPs have called for a nationwide conversation to tackle the growing number of deaths caused by cancer in the country. Chairperson of the National Assembly Committee, James Nyikal (Sem...

item.title: Africa’s Shift to Locally Made Solar-Powered Electric Motorbikes
item.description: Motorcycles are vital in Africa, not just for transport but as livelihoods. Kenya alone has 1.5 million riders, yet less than 0.1% of the 27 million bikes in sub-Saharan Africa are electric. A team fr...

item.title: iKON Announces Manila Stop for Their 'iKON Fourever' Tour
item.description: K-pop fans in the Philippines have a reason to celebrate as the popular South Korean boy group iKON has officially included Manila in their upcoming 'iKON Fourever' world tour. This tour promises to s...

item.title: World Bank Launches Programs to Boost Skills and Social Support for Over Three Million Tanzanians
item.description: The World Bank has greenlit two new programs aimed at supporting over three million Tanzanians in key areas of education, job skills, and social protection. The Second Education and Skills for Product...

item.title: Kwankwasiyya Movement Members Directed to Join ADC Following Kwankwaso
item.description: Di Kano-based Kwankwasiyya Movement don tell all im members for every part of Nigeria make dem register with di African Democratic Congress (ADC) as dem leader, Senator Rabiu Kwankwaso, don decide to ...

item.title: Tanzania Highlights Investment-Friendly Mining Policies to Boost Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania government is actively promoting its mining sector as an attractive destination for both local and foreign investors. In recent talks with a delegation from China's Liaoning Fangda Group, the...

item.title: Key South African News Updates: Fuel Prices, Child Amputation Case, and Wastewater Crisis
item.description: This morning's top news in South Africa covers three pressing issues affecting citizens across the country. First, more than 500,000 households that depend on paraffin for energy will face a substanti...

item.title: Police Investigate Threatening Call Made to Bracebridge Elementary School
item.description: Barrie Police are investigating a threatening phone call made to an elementary school in Bracebridge, Ontario. The call has raised concerns among parents, staff, and local authorities regarding the sa...
